stories.text:
Once upon a quiet town. There was an owl who learned things quietly. Every star made him think. A curious thought. He wondered how cats dream.

One autumn night. He fluttered downstairs. Saw the moon light cats' claws.

They wouldn't eat fish. Fancy some mice. So he'd fly about. One day. Offer.

They heard. They hissed. Cats.

But no.

One day.

One mouse.

He flew back home. Hung upside-down.

They ate. Laughed. Said owl ate nothing. They ignored him. Until...

He waited. Waited. Waited.

They wanted eggs.

So he slept.

Until tomorrow morning.

Oh.

Maybe cheese?

They liked cheese.

He got cheese. They loved.

Talk owl-talk. Owls sleep.

Happy owl.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

You are in a library. What happens? You look up.

There is a giant dragon. Why do you think that? Perhaps it can breathe something really scary. Won't think twice.

You run away. Maybe climb a tree.


You decide you could use some quiet. Find a bookshelf. Sit quiet. Quiet. That's it.

Suddenly.

You look up!!

They have some lovely birds.



You like them?!

Sure.



Take one home.

You do love birds.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There once was an old owl who lived deep in Whispering Woods. Each evening she would watch as fireflies danced in the twilight sky. One evening her eyes grew sleepy. She thought perhaps she'd lie down and drift off.

Suddenly, there was a tapping!

Tap! tap!

She squawked awake. Someone was there.

Just then, fluffy white mice scampered down her nose.

"Oh! There you are!"

They squeaked happy tunes.

Maybe foxes?

There were lots! One big one took their tails.

They danced!

What luck!

At dawn, curious mice came back. They chased the foxes away!

Maybe worms?

That's strange.

It certainly was. Next day...

A shadow! Fangs!

No foxes.

That could've chewed their ears off.

But maybe...

Maybe owls?

They swooped. One eyed her fiercely.

No more tapping. Never again.

Just flies.

You slept well. Now watch the sky. Bright stars!

That owl

There once upon a time in a lush forest. There was a pond. And there lived a wise old owl. Everyday I'd watch him swim gracefully. I loved owls. He didn't eat fish though. Just berries. I had some. I sat. Quiet.

That night was dark. Nothing scared me. Just shadows.

There. A shiny thing. Light.

I flew. No feathers. Clothes. Glasses.

Thoughts. Maybe I shouldn’t?

You think? You think I'll drop it? There ain't anything that scares me.

That shiny thing.

I flew closer. I could hold it?

Nothing. Stars just float.

Wow. Owls think. Want?

Nope.

But that light shines.

I sat.

That night.




I know.



Such big feelings.


What do owls know?Think that.



Oh.



A book.




That was enough.





You okay?

Just think?


That book.

Such things.

There just might know owls.





I know.




How

There was an owl.

That was an owl who didn't think much. But one day it decided. Well. Decide. It wanted to fly south. It would. So it flew.

It flew a lot.

Then one day. Wow. It's autumn. Lots of leaves. I flew.

I flew south.

It got cold.

I flew south warmer.

Eventually I stopped.

Even winters are okay.

I've seen animals.

That's enough for now. I'll think about it.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

A man lost. You think people don't notice when people disappear? Of course they notice. You think? Just look.

There is my favorite diner. You should stop there sometime.

It has blue booths. Sometimes I sit alone.

Sometimes my wife. Always my kids.

The waitress knows me.

The other day I said hello.

Later she smiled. It was like smiling at my daughter.

That girl smiled.

I like my stories.



It rains. I think.







It always rains.

You'd think.

A dark sky.

I walk.





I always walk.

Nothing happens.

Just rain.



I have a thing.

It listens.





I go.




I think.







You think?




You're right.




I think I'll climb.





So I walk.

Maybe I'll find my girl.



You never think?



That's enough.





Just my boy.





We watch.




You think.







You think.







You think.




A book.







No.



Just stars.




You miss stars.

That's alright.





I go

Once upon a time, there was an old tree. No leaves ever fell from it. Just blobs. People thought it odd. They chopped it down. One branch. Nothing happened. Then another branch. Nothing. They cut it all down. Nothing.

But birds knew. So did squirrels. They whispered.

Then one summer. Something big. Birds fell. Squirrels buried nuts. Something big came. They knew. And ran.

It was a spaceship. They had come. Lots of light.

You're home. They said.

They took them home.

What is this?

You're beautiful.

They lived many years. No more stars. But something else.

Something new.

One night.

They dream. Together.

Their grandchildren come.

They laugh. Fly.

They'll always have stars. Everywhere. Forever.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

I love cats. I think cats are the best thing alive. You just never know.

What'd I see one time? Two! Sitting together watching me eat. They were kind of scared. But adorable. So shiny. So orange.




You know cats. You never think they listen. You think you know. Nope. You think you love. Cats think they knew you.







And then? Oh man. I loved my orange kitty.







One night. There was snow. I sat under my rosemary bush. I was reading. Then I heard a crunch.

A crunch? I'm imagining. Snow. Sitting. Crunch. There.




I jumped.




I had owls.




I got orange.





There wasn't much room.







Just a burrowing owl.




Oh wow.




I never get lonely.







But I know I laugh.



That's my pet sock?





I don't know. Isn't there some stuff about that?They love socks.





I wear it.




They look nice.



It might help

There was once a guy named Bob.

Bob loved trees. One day he went to this shady forest. There were huge elm trees!

Bob decided to climb one.

But first....

A fox!

Bob ran!

But a bird!

Bob flew!

Then he climbed. He reached the highest elm. There was a big branch.

Bob climbed all the way! Wow!!

All of the bark was orange!

That night bob made a ladder of leaves. Climbed down. Bob loved those orange bark trees. Just thought. He leaves them some treats.

Now Bob thinks squirrels like those treats.

Bob loves squirrels!<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Once upon a time in a little village nestled in greenleaf woods.

There lived a boy named darwin smith. He loved beetles. So obsessed.

One summer. Darwy knew birds.

One afternoon he found cicadas.

Now cicadas love trees.

That's where Darwy saw a branch.

He tapped.

A cicada buzz.

It talked.

You fly?

You talk?

Well my wings...

They didn't work.

Later Darwy built clubs.

He knew he could change worlds.




With every letter.

It got big.

Soon towns.

And cities.

People were quiet.

At night birds sang.

They stopped. Fade.

And nothing sounded.

Except Darwy.




I fly?

How?

They sing.

Darwin just smiled.




It grows.

Days.

Into summers.

And winters.

It goes. Sing.

Sing stars?

Maybe.



But one summer.

A voice.

A red robin.

Just said.


So darwy listened.



Just think.

Maybe I could do that.

Maybe fly

Once upon a time there was a fisherman who went out into the ocean each morning. Each morning he took two worms. He would reel in his line just so because hooks have claws. He had dozens! He held one worm aloft. He shook his head thinking how one wouldn. Perhaps two. He worked tirelessly. His rod shook. Maybe it snapped! There! Nothing!

That night a fox spotted him. He didn't dare eat worm because the fox gnawed them away. He chased him. Fox ran faster. Sailor just swayed. Finally fox stopped dead!

Why? Maybe kill you?

Thinking nothing more than that.

Fox pawed him.

How?

His hands. Just hands? You think?! You just bite?

I guess.

Fox looked thoughtful.

You could!

Well...I go home.
You stay?

Maybe.

You'll live?

I survive. Maybe grow old dreaming?


You think?


That sounds good...

I think. Think long.




That's ok.




I

Once upon a quiet autumn afternoon, there was a quaint little town called Oak Grove. Inside every house stood a tiny oak tree. One year especially special oak tree fell leaves spun gold. Children loved chasing those shimmering leaves. One such child was Lucy.

Lucy would fill her red sock full of them and carry home tales swapped with friends.

One day thick fog drifted through town. A shadow rose. Birds fluttered loudly. And then big boots landed.

"Oh hello there!" boots said.

With courage Lucy told beasts. Boots wanted wood, leaf hats. She offered her sock.

Big toe admired. Leather wrinkles. Boots smiled.

She gave. Boots carried safe back home.

But one moonlight night a whisper prowled.

A ghost bird swooped. Lucy clutching sock. Laughing scared ghost away.

Oldest oak smiled secret leaf.

Long afterwards children learned ghost feared oak owl.

They danced under. Owls swooped bright.

Every year oak birds. Leaves spun gold spun owl delight

Two cats sat under an oak tree.

One said:"I think I'll hunt for mice."

The other said:"More cheese?"

They hunted together. They liked cheese. You could hear them munch.

They went deeper into the woods. Creatures hid. Eventually...

One paw caught something cool!

It was a moth. They danced together.

That night stars twinkled.

That night they dreamed.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There once was a young girl named Lily. She loved butterflies. Every rainy afternoon she would go outside. She'd swing gracefully. That's when she saw them—aflutter. She'd watch. Slow-motion wings. Life-changing.

Lily learned magic. Whenever butterflies appeared. She'd pet them. They'd float.

Until one day a wise owl. Said. If you steal butterfly wings...they die. That's why.

She thought. Maybe I shouldn't. That would be cruel.

But butterflies. Wanted her feathers. Said whispers.

So she took them. Nothing.

For weeks.

Every dusk.

Just butterflies.

She saved them. That's all. So grateful.

Her favourite thing remained. Butterflies. Everywhere she went. They seemed happy.

It made her think. Maybe everything needs love.

That owl...might've changed something. Forever?

I think?<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There was an old wise owl. Each morning it would hunt insects. One day it met an owl.

"You look lost," thought owl. It kept going.

Later that day owl saw some berries. They tasted good. It ate berries.

Another day owl heard laughter.

It flew closer. There were little creatures playing.

They had fun together forever. Then owl died. Friends remembered. Stories told. So owl lives forever. Because laughter.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Once upon a moonlit night, a wise old owl sat atop her branch. She thought of stories.

She spun one about a mermaid queen sailing through icy depths. Her tales enchanted sea creatures.

One turtle dove.

The owl couldn't stop. Stories flowed forever.

A castle rose. Creatures danced.

Another tale spun.

A cat discovered three paw prints.

Something snuck away.

That night thoughts became reality.

A gust swept. Leaves danced.

A wise owl had tales.

She settled down.

Peacefully snoozing.







I think I love you.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There once was an old owl. He lived high up in woodland.

One sunny summer day he fell asleep. Perhaps from dreams.

Birds fluttered by. Maybe teasing. They chattered cruelly.

But sleepily he dreamed. Maybe gods? Surely.

They welcomed him. A realm unknown.

A palace shining. An eternal joy.

A hundred souls singing.

Or just one. Whispering softly.

Maybe that was best.

He would know his way.

That night he woke. Alone. Sad.

He listened. Nothing.

Maybe wisdom. Or folly?

He flew away. Only knew.

The end.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There was a very strange cat. It could transform. Into anything. It could turn into a dog. I think it was my favorite. It loved my walks. One day I took my dog out. There was a coyote. It ran fast. I ran faster. I fell. My dog ran away. I got up slowly. It couldn't run. I walked home. That's how I knew.

It liked my books. I read them often. It liked my thoughts. After dark I'd think thoughts loud.




I think I love moths.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Once upon an autumn day. There was an old owl perched atop Whispering Willow tree.

She stared blankly up at clouds flecked white. Whispers danced.

It grew dark. Stars danced. She imagined flying. The moonlight felt soft.

Moonshine danced through.

Night came. Owls? No. Thoughts.

Dream.

She flew softly. Stars. Moonshine. Quiet. Rest. Dream. Wake. Silent thoughts.

She fell. Owlfriends?

Thought.

They gathered sleepy.

There! Her friends. Birds.

Together! Singing.

Whispering secrets.

Then hush. Down.

They flew.

Tomorrow. Dream once more.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Willowbrook, there lived a curious hedgehog named Spike. Every morning Spike liked to trot through Willowbrook garden centres admiring wildflowers. He knew all sorts of animals. There was Oliver Owl fluttering about high trees. Benny Bear snoozed quietly beneath giant sequoias.

One rainy afternoon Spike saw something unusual. Four kittens sat beside what looked like an umbrella. They played peek-a-thumb. Spike felt soft. Perhaps he should join.

With gentle steps, Spike climbed closer. That's friendship. He patted theirs.

Then came slippery times. Cats! One spit. Spike scurried.

No harm done. Friendship.

Every day they chased raindrops.

Years went by. The kittens grew. One day Willowbrook grew louder.

They flew away.

Just adventures.

Years later.

The kittens came home.

The hedgehog greeted them.

Now friends old.

Together.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>

There was once an old man wearing glasses. Something always happened whenever he read. He wondered why.

One day he went deep exploring somewhere dark. Lost. Crawling through tunnels.

Then he heard strange whispers.

They told him secrets.

He got scared.

Something followed him.

There's nothing there.

He turned.

Nothin'.

Just darkness.
